 # [#8215: Feature: Emit separate "thinking" stream in agent events for real-time reasoning visibility](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8215)
@@ -86,10 +87,21 @@ Current `main` already emits reasoning as a dedicated `thinking` agent event and
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing from a Codex review. Current `main` already emits reasoning as a dedicated `thinking` agent event from `emitReasoningStream()` in `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts:706`, includes `thinking` in the agent-event stream contract in `src/infra/agent-events.ts:5`, and forwards that event to WebSocket listeners through the gateway agent-event broadcast path in `src/gateway/server-chat.ts:965`. I verified this against `main` at `b2cface9d5fd048a514d82292a5baee7190e0ee3`.
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
 
-I could not prove that this has shipped in a tagged release yet, so I am leaving the release field blank. If there is still follow-up needed for the built-in Control UI to render live `thinking` events, that looks like separate UI work rather than a missing core agent-event emission.
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd048a514d82292a5baee7190e0ee3). Fix evidence: commit [b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd048a514d82292a5baee7190e0ee3).
+Current `main` already emits reasoning as a dedicated `thinking` agent event and forwards it through the gateway agent-event path to WebSocket listeners. The remaining gap I found is UI consumption in the built-in Control UI, not missing core event emission.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Reasoning stream emits `thinking` agent events:** `emitReasoningStream()` now computes a reasoning delta and calls `emitAgentEvent({ runId, stream: "thinking", data: { text, delta } })` before invoking `onReasoningStream`. This is the exact backend change requested in the issue body. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts:706`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/b2cface9d5fd/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts#L706), [b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd))
+- **`thinking` is a first-class agent-event stream type:** The agent event stream union explicitly includes `"thinking"`, so the event is part of the current mainline contract rather than an ad hoc payload. ([`src/infra/agent-events.ts:5`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/b2cface9d5fd/src/infra/agent-events.ts#L5), [b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd))
+- **Gateway forwards non-tool agent events to WS clients:** `createAgentEventHandler()` broadcasts non-tool events through the `agent` channel, so `thinking` events emitted above are forwarded to subscribed WebSocket listeners. ([`src/gateway/server-chat.ts:965`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/b2cface9d5fd/src/gateway/server-chat.ts#L965), [b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd))
+- **Built-in UI still ignores non-tool agent streams:** The current Control UI agent-event handler returns early for any stream other than `compaction`, `lifecycle`, `fallback`, or `tool`. That means any remaining work is UI-side rendering, not missing backend emission. ([`ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts:450`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/b2cface9d5fd/ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts#L450), [b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd))
+- **Not proven shipped in a tagged release:** No tag in this checkout contains the current `main` SHA, so I cannot tie this implementation to a released version. ([b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd048a514d82292a5baee7190e0ee3); fix evidence: commit [b2cface9d5fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/b2cface9d5fd048a514d82292a5baee7190e0ee3).

 # [#8231: [Feature]: terminal-based web search](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8231)
@@ -90,8 +91,22 @@ Current `main` already provides terminal web search and key-free web-search prov
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing this after a Codex review because current `main` already ships the requested capability. OpenClaw has a terminal web-search surface via `openclaw infer web search` (`src/cli/capability-cli.ts:1752`, `docs/cli/infer.md:91`), and the managed `web_search` feature now supports key-free providers, including DuckDuckGo (`docs/tools/web.md:63`, `docs/tools/web.md:178`, `docs/tools/duckduckgo-search.md:10`, `extensions/duckduckgo/src/ddg-search-provider.ts:36`). I also confirmed the same files are present in the shipped `v2026.4.22` release (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`). This is not packaged as a separately named new core skill, but the requested outcome, terminal-based web search without requiring a Brave API key, is already implemented.
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current `main` already provides terminal web search and key-free web-search providers, so the request in #8231 is implemented.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Terminal web-search CLI exists:** `openclaw infer web search` is implemented as a first-party CLI command under the `web search` capability. ([`src/cli/capability-cli.ts:1752`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/cli/capability-cli.ts#L1752), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Infer docs describe the headless terminal surface:** Docs call `openclaw infer` the canonical headless surface and explicitly document `openclaw infer web search --query ... --json`. Public docs: [`docs/cli/infer.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/infer). ([`docs/cli/infer.md:9`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/infer), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Key-free web-search providers are documented on main:** Web-search docs list DuckDuckGo as a key-free fallback and show key-free fallback auto-detection after API-backed providers. Public docs: [`docs/tools/web.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/web). ([`docs/tools/web.md:63`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/web), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **DuckDuckGo provider docs require no API key:** Dedicated provider docs state that DuckDuckGo is a key-free `web_search` provider with no API key or account required, configurable via `openclaw configure --section web`. Public docs: [`docs/tools/duckduckgo-search.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/duckduckgo-search). ([`docs/tools/duckduckgo-search.md:10`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/duckduckgo-search), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Bundled no-key provider is implemented in code:** The bundled DuckDuckGo provider registers a `web_search` tool described as returning titles, URLs, and snippets with no API key required. ([`extensions/duckduckgo/src/ddg-search-provider.ts:36`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/extensions/duckduckgo/src/ddg-search-provider.ts#L36), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Feature is already shipped in the latest tagged release:** The same CLI/docs/provider files are present in tag `v2026.4.22`, so this is not only on unreleased main. ([00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2); fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

 # [#8255: [Feature]: post-write hooks for workspace files](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8255)
@@ -91,8 +92,22 @@ Issue #8255 requests optional post-write automation for workspace files. Current
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing this as a ClawHub/plugin-scope request based on a Codex review of current `main`. The project direction explicitly says optional capability should usually ship as plugins and that the bar for adding optional capability to core is intentionally high (`VISION.md:54`). The automation surface already routes this kind of extension through hooks and plugin-managed hook packs (`docs/automation/hooks.md:9`, `docs/automation/hooks.md:129`, `docs/cli/hooks.md:197`), and community capabilities are expected to ship via ClawHub or npm (`docs/plugins/community.md:9`). A post-write auto-commit/encrypt/sync/custom-script feature fits that extension surface better than adding arbitrary post-write command execution to OpenClaw core. If you build it as a community plugin or hook pack, ClawHub is the intended distribution path.
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a510758645c5267734b984f4885a605b67). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.
+Closing this as better suited for [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/)/community plugin work after Codex review.
+
+Issue #8255 requests optional post-write automation for workspace files. Current main direction keeps optional automation out of core and routes it through plugins/hook packs, so this belongs in the ClawHub/community-plugin surface rather than OpenClaw core.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Vision: optional capability should stay out of core:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says OpenClaw has an extensive plugin API, core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/VISION.md#L54), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Existing automation surface is hook/plugin based:** Docs describe hooks as event-driven automation inside the Gateway, explicitly state hooks can be bundled inside plugins, and document plugin hooks plus hook-pack installation rather than new core config knobs for arbitrary external actions. Public docs: [`docs/automation/hooks.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/hooks). ([`docs/automation/hooks.md:9`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/hooks), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Hook packs install through plugins:** CLI docs route hook-pack installation through `openclaw plugins install`, reinforcing that operator automation is expected to ship as installable plugin/hook-pack packages. Public docs: [`docs/cli/hooks.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/hooks). ([`docs/cli/hooks.md:197`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/hooks), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Community distribution path is ClawHub or npm:** Community plugin docs say third-party capabilities should be published on ClawHub or npm and installed with `openclaw plugins install`, with ClawHub as the canonical discovery surface. Public docs: [`docs/plugins/community.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/community). ([`docs/plugins/community.md:9`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/community), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Maintainer automation already has a third-party-plugin close path:** The repo's auto-response workflow includes a dedicated close path telling contributors to make this kind of capability as a third-party plugin maintained in their own repo, then add it to the community plugins page. ([`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml:81`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/.github/workflows/auto-response.yml#L81), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Checkout remained clean:** Read-only inspection only; `git status --short` returned no changes. ([c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+
+So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a510758645c5267734b984f4885a605b67).

 # [#8263: Feature Request: Webchat User Display Name Customization](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8263)
@@ -91,8 +92,22 @@ Current `main` already implements Control UI user display-name customization via
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on current `main`. Codex review confirmed that Control UI now supports a browser-local personal identity for the operator: Quick Settings exposes an editable `Name` field in `ui/src/ui/views/config-quick.ts:454`, the value is persisted via `ui/src/ui/storage.ts:281`, and chat rendering uses that configured name for user-message attribution in `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:298`. The behavior is also covered by tests in `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts:310` and `ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts:447`, and documented in `docs/web/control-ui.md:79`. I could not verify a released tag that already shipped this, so I’m only marking it implemented on `main`, not assigning a fixed release.
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
+
+Current `main` already implements Control UI user display-name customization via a browser-local personal identity, with UI wiring, persistence, rendering, docs, and tests all present on HEAD.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Quick Settings exposes editable personal name:** The Control UI Quick Settings includes a `Personal` card with a `Name` text input, preview, clear action, and `This browser only` copy for the local user identity. ([`ui/src/ui/views/config-quick.ts:454`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/views/config-quick.ts#L454), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Identity is stored as browser-local user state:** Local user identity is loaded from and saved to the dedicated `openclaw.control.user.v1` local-storage key, separate from gateway settings. ([`ui/src/ui/storage.ts:281`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/storage.ts#L281), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **App state wires the local user identity through the UI:** The app boots `userName`/`userAvatar` state from local identity and passes it into both Quick Settings and chat rendering; updates flow through `applyLocalUserIdentity`. ([`ui/src/ui/app.ts:167`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/app.ts#L167), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Chat rendering uses the configured user name:** Message-group rendering resolves `opts.userName` through `resolveLocalUserName(...)` and uses that value for user-message attribution instead of a hardcoded generic label. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:298`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts#L298), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Behavior is covered by tests:** Tests assert that user message footers render a configured local user name, and storage tests verify persistence/clearing of the local identity. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts:310`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts#L310), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Docs and changelog describe the feature:** Control UI docs now document a per-browser personal identity (display name and avatar), and the changelog records the feature under `Unreleased`. Public docs: [`docs/web/control-ui.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/web/control-ui). ([`docs/web/control-ui.md:79`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/web/control-ui), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).

 # [#8266: Feature Request: Log tool call arguments in debug logs](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8266)
@@ -91,8 +92,22 @@ The request is still not implemented in core debug log strings on current `main`
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing this as `clawhub` after a Codex review. Current `main` still keeps the low-level debug line minimal in `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:604`, so this specific core log-format change is not implemented there. But the observability data the request wants already lives on the plugin/hook surface that OpenClaw expects external integrations to use: tool start emits structured `args` in `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:608`, and `after_tool_call` exposes `params` plus `result` in the public hook contract at `src/plugins/hook-types.ts:339`, wired from `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:1122`. That matches the project scope in `VISION.md:54`, which says core stays lean and optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and it matches existing logging/audit precedent in the bundled `command-logger` hook at `src/hooks/bundled/command-logger/HOOK.md:16`. For richer external monitoring, the intended path is a ClawHub/plugin hook that writes its own structured log stream rather than expanding core debug logs.
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a510758645c5267734b984f4885a605b67). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.
+Closing this as better suited for [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/)/community plugin work after Codex review.
+
+The request is still not implemented in core debug log strings on current `main`, but OpenClaw already exposes tool arguments/results through the plugin hook surface intended for external observability, so this belongs as a plugin/ClawHub integration rather than a core logging change.
+
+What I checked:
+
+- **Core debug log remains minimal:** Current `main` still logs tool start/end with only runId, tool name, and toolCallId; it does not append tool args/results to the debug log line. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:604`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts#L604), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Tool args already emitted on start:** The same handler emits a structured tool event containing `args`, which an external observer can consume without changing core debug log formatting. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:608`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts#L608), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Public hook contract exposes params and result:** `after_tool_call` is a public plugin hook with `params`, `result`, `error`, and `durationMs`, matching the observability payload requested by the issue. ([`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:339`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/plugins/hook-types.ts#L339), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Embedded runner invokes after_tool_call with params/result:** Current runner code passes adjusted tool params and sanitized result into `after_tool_call`, so a plugin can write structured monitoring logs today. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:1122`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts#L1122), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Project scope prefers plugins for optional capability:** The repo [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says core should stay lean and optional capability should usually ship as plugins, which fits external monitoring/observability. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/VISION.md#L54), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Logging/audit precedent already lives in hooks:** The bundled `command-logger` hook writes structured audit logs for monitoring/debugging, showing this class of feature is already modeled as a hook/plugin responsibility. ([`src/hooks/bundled/command-logger/HOOK.md:16`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/hooks/bundled/command-logger/HOOK.md#L16), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+
+So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
+

([`docs/tools/subagents.md:10`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/subagents), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **sessions_spawn uses the standard agent run path:** `spawnSubagentDirect()` starts the child by calling gateway method `agent` with the child `sessionKey`, so spawned work goes through the same agent runtime as any other session. ([`src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts:969`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts#L969), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Embedded runs apply compaction settings for every session:** The embedded attempt path applies the auto-compaction guard and then reapplies compaction settings from config after resource loading. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1055`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L1055), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Safeguard compaction is wired into embedded runs:** When compaction is in safeguard mode, embedded runs register the compaction safeguard extension for the session manager. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.ts:89`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.ts#L89), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Docs say auto-compaction is session-default behavior:** Compaction docs state that auto-compaction is on by default for sessions when they near the context limit or overflow. Public docs: [`docs/concepts/compaction.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/compaction). ([`docs/concepts/compaction.md:29`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/compaction), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Latest release contains the same behavior:** The latest release tag already contains the standard embedded-run compaction setup and the session-based subagent model. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:1055`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts#L1055), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376))

+- **Outbound text now always uses Feishu post payloads:** `buildFeishuPostMessagePayload()` always returns `msgType: "post"`, and `sendMessageFeishu()` always uses that payload for normal text sends. (`extensions/feishu/src/send.ts:432`)

([`src/agents/system-prompt.ts:401`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/system-prompt.ts#L401), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Docs describe the documentation section as agent context:** The system-prompt docs state that the prompt includes the local OpenClaw docs directory and instructs the model to consult local docs first. Public docs: [`docs/concepts/system-prompt.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/system-prompt). ([`docs/concepts/system-prompt.md:201`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/system-prompt), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Gateway dashboard is documented directly:** There is a dedicated dashboard doc that defines the gateway dashboard as the browser Control UI and covers access/auth details. Public docs: [`docs/web/dashboard.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/web/dashboard). ([`docs/web/dashboard.md:8`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/web/dashboard), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Agent workflow explicitly uses docs lookup:** The CLI/TUI docs tell users to have the embedded agent compare config against docs and show `!openclaw docs gateway auth token secretref` as part of the repair loop. Public docs: [`docs/cli/config.md`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/config). ([`docs/cli/config.md:404`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/config), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Behavior is covered by tests:** System-prompt tests assert that the prompt includes the docs path and the instruction to consult local docs first. ([`src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts:394`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts#L394), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Shipped release also contains the implementation:** The latest release commit includes the same docs-section logic in [`src/agents/system-prompt.ts`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376/src/agents/system-prompt.ts), so this is not just unreleased `main` behavior. ([`src/agents/system-prompt.ts:401`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376/src/agents/system-prompt.ts#L401), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
